description: Remove unreachable response.ok checks in fetchChunk() and getQueryStatus(), and remove the exported but never-called getQueryStatus() function from snowflake-api.ts.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/snowflake && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Remove dead response.ok check in fetchChunk() and delete unused getQueryStatus()
userStories.description: In plugins/snowflake/src/snowflake-api.ts, (1) fetchChunk() has `if (!response.ok)` at line 273 after fetchFromPage, which is unreachable dead code; (2) getQueryStatus() is exported at line 376 but never imported or called by any tool — it is dead code. Remove the dead ok-check from fetchChunk() and delete the getQueryStatus() function entirely.
acceptanceCriteria: The `if (!response.ok)` block in fetchChunk() is removed
The getQueryStatus() function and its export are deleted from snowflake-api.ts
No other file imports or references getQueryStatus()
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/snowflake/src/snowflake-api.ts (remove dead ok-check in fetchChunk at line 273, delete getQueryStatus at line 376)

Context:
fetchChunk dead code (lines 273-275):
  if (!response.ok) { // DEAD — fetchFromPage threw already
    throw ToolError.internal(`Chunk fetch failed: ${response.status} — ${response.statusText}`);
  }

getQueryStatus function (lines 376-400) — exported but never called. Confirm by checking all tool files in plugins/snowflake/src/tools/ for any import of getQueryStatus. If not imported anywhere, delete it.

Note: The `if (!response.ok)` in getQueryStatus (line 388) is also dead code, but since the entire function is being deleted, it goes away automatically.

Constraints:
- Do NOT touch the snowflake query execution function at lines 196-225 (which uses raw fetch() and has a LIVE response.ok check — that is intentional because it uses raw fetch, not fetchFromPage)
- After deleting getQueryStatus, verify with grep that no tool file imports it before confirming the deletion is safe
